repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Office of the Vice Provost

did.abstract: This collection contains the papers and publications of James Spencer ranging from his student years Cornell to well past his retirement in 1987.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: This collection contains the papers and publications of James Spencer ranging from his student years at Cornell until well past his retirement in 1987. Included are state budget records and other records relating to S.U.N.Y. the Commission on the Future of the Cornell Cooperative Extension Service with Robben Fleming as the chair, the Cornell Commission on Free Speech, and correspondence of James W. Spencer and M.C. Nesheim. Spencer's speeches publications, including his Master's thesis, are also included in this collection. Also present are the daily notes Spencer kept from 1951 until 2019, as well as autobiographical data sheets compiled by Dr. Spencer reflecting on his life, achievements, and influences.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: James W. Spencer Papers, #4-2-2136. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Boxes 1, 6, 7 and 10 restricted to the permission of the Office of the Vice Provost.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James Wendell Spencer (BCE 1949, MCE 1951) served many roles at Cornell prior to his retirement in 1987. Spencer began as an Assistant Professor shortly after receiving his MCE in 1951, eventually becoming Professor in Highway Engineering, a position he held from 1961 until 1987. In 1967 he took a sabbatical in order to receive his PhD from Stanford University. From August 1970 until July 1973 he served as Vice Director of the Cooperative Extension. Spencer was made Dean of the NYS College of Agriculture and Life Sciences in 1973, a position he held until becoming Special Assistant to the President in 1978. He became Vice Provost in 1979, a position he would hold until his retirement in 1987.

After Spencer's retirement as Professor and Vice Provost, he served for a year as a part-time consultant to the President. From 1988 until 1989 he was the Director of Occupational Health and Safety. In 1990 he became a program officer at the Atlantic Philanthropic Services (later known as the Atlantic Philanthropies), and retired in 2001. His papers from his years at Atlantic can be found in the Atlantic Philanthropies Archives, #8540.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: The Atlantic Philanthropies Archives, #8540; Columbia University Atlantic Philanthropies oral history project, #8737

controlaccess.subject: Freedom of speech.
controlaccess.geogname: New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Commission on Free Speech

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Series I. James W. Spencer SUNY files
Series II. Vice Provost James W. Spencer correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ag. Ec., Ag. Eng., Agronomy, An. Sci., Education, Food Science

did.unittitle: Series III. University unit plans and reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports, plans, and background material on planning within various university departments and colleges.

did.unittitle: Reports: Managing Decline - 1978; Land-Grant Mission of the University - 1974
Herbert Johnson Museum of Art - Advisory Council
Historic information - Academic I. General pt. 2
Historic information - Academic I. Dining Hall
Series IV. Commission on Free Speech memos and reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memos and reports from Spencer's tenure as member of the Cornell Presidential Commission on Free Speech ((December 1986-March 1987) while Vice Provost.

did.unittitle: Series V. Fleming Commission: Future of the Cornell Cooperative Extension Service

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memos, reports, and publications from Spencer's tenure as president's liasion with the Commission on the Future of the Cornell Cooperative Extension (June 1986-March 1987)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robben W. Wright was Chair of the Commission.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Spencer's publications, chiefly in the areas of low-cost roads and highway engineering. Also included are two laboratory research reports he wrote while a Cornell graduate student in civil engineering, and his 1951 thesis Effective County Highway Department Practices in New York State. Of note is a 1963 report written with Dale Corson and Robert Fehr to the Ford Foundation on the subject of engineering education in Colombia. In addition to articles written by Spencer, this series contains relevant publications including journals such as Highway Superindendents' Digest. Also included in this series are Spencer's speeches. Originally identified as speeches he gave after he left Riley Robb Hall in 1970, the files contain speeches dating back to 1967.

did.unittitle: "An Approach to the Study of Local Road Management." Reprinted from Highway Research Board Proceedings, vol. 38
"The Back to School Movement." From New York State County Highway Superintendents' Association

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Location and Evaluation of Gravel Sources for Highway Use: Short Course, co-written with Olin K. Dart, Jr.

Newfield Test Road: Observations on the Performance of a Stabilized Run-of-Bank Gravel Road

scopecontent.p: Notebook from Spencer's time as a student. Includes notes on soil and county research.

Summary of Proceedings at School for Highway Superintendents, June 18, 19, 20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to Spencer from the National Research Council.

did.unittitle: Types and Characteristics of Bituminous Materials: a "Programmed Learning" Text for Highway Superintendents
"What is Needed For All-Weather Roads and Driveways," from Hoard's Dairyman
"What is the Role of the County Engineering in Highway Planning," from Better Roads
Series VII. Daily notes and call logs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daily notes that Spencer kept from 1951-1956, 1964, 1966 until 2019. Gaps correpsond with his sabbaticals at the University of California, Berkeley (September 1957-March 1958) and Stanford University (September 1964-June 1966). While the notes initially started as a calender, tracking Spencer's daily activities, in 1966 they chiefly summarized phone conversations and meetings, both professional and personal. The notes span the entirety of Spencer's career at Cornell, and subsequent tenure at the Atlantic Philanthropies as senior progam officer for Higher Education and the Aging and Health programs. Oftentimes memos and meeting agendas were taped onto notebook pages. This series also contains call logs Spencer kept from 1973 until 1987, in which he recorded the dates he spoke to various individuals.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Notebooks arranged chronologically, followed by the 1973-1987 call logs.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Notebooks from 1966 through 1974 were labelled by Spencer as "time guide record". Subsequent, unlabelled books were given this title by the archivist to follow suit. Personal information, including medical details and social security numbers, were redacted or removed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General files chiefly related to Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides taken by Spencer when field engineer for Donald J. Belcher and Asscociates. In this role, Spencer studied engineering characteristics of soils related to selection of site for the city of Brasilia.

did.unittitle: Cornell Chronicle - list of articles featuring Spencer
Cornell Higher Education Research Institute (CHERI) Policy Research Conference: Assessing Public Higher Education at the Start of the 21st Century
Cornell Higher Education Research Institute (CHERI) 2006-2007 Policy Research Conference: Doctoral Education and the Faculty of the Future
Cornell South African divestment protests -- newsclippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains photographs.

scopecontent.p: Folder contains photographs.
